title: Fastify v5 over v4 for apps/storage
rationale: Fastify v4 EOL June 2025. v5.8.x pinned in apps/storage. Phases 5 and 6 inherit silently — risk if a task manually reinstalls with wrong version. Add Fastify version assertion to Phase 5/6 pre-work checklists.

description: COOP/COEP headers required for SharedArrayBuffer break cross-origin tile CDNs (Cloudflare R2, tile.openfreemap.org). Tiles fail to load when isolation headers set.
resolution: Use postMessage at 30Hz for cross-thread coord sync. Avoids SharedArrayBuffer and COOP/COEP requirement entirely.

description: yarn add <pkg> in a workspace can re-resolve transitive deps and de-hoist a root-level devDep that was previously hoisted to top-level node_modules. Wave 3b instance: 'yarn add @playwright/test' to atlas-app caused 'vite-plugin-checker' (declared in root code/package.json devDeps and used by excalidraw-app/vite.config.mts) to be displaced; subsequent typecheck failed with 'Cannot find module vite-plugin-checker'. Worker 15 mislabeled it 'pre-existing baseline' and reported DONE. The error was real and new.
resolution: After ANY yarn add in a workspace, re-run yarn workspace-level typecheck/build at the orchestrator level. If a typecheck fails with a module-not-found error in an UNRELATED workspace, run 'yarn install' once at the monorepo root before deeper debugging — this re-hoists displaced deps. Only escalate if the error persists post-reseat.

description: pre-commit secret-scan false-positive on vendored WASM binary — expected clean pass, got 'potential secret detected' on code/packages/excalidraw/subset/harfbuzz/harfbuzz-wasm.ts:57 (base64-encoded harfbuzz WASM for font subsetting). Hook has no allowlist mechanism; only escape is --no-verify after explicit user authorization.
resolution: used --no-verify with user authorization; no allowlist path exists in hook today

content: vendored fork .git backup: when inlining an upstream fork (option C), move embedded .git via mv to a sibling location, NOT rm. Reversibility costs 35MB; VENDOR.md pins upstream commit for re-sync. Backup safe to delete only after first push to remote is verified.

description: seeds CLI flag misspelling — expected --label and priority 'medium', got generic error. Correct flags: --labels (plural), priority value 'P2'. CLI does not auto-correct or suggest alternatives.

content: GitHub repo creation one-shot: rename master→main BEFORE gh repo create (still local, no remote, fully safe). Then: gh repo create <name> --public --source=. --remote=origin --push. Verify auth first with gh auth status. Order matters — branch rename after push requires force-push.

name: anti-pattern-catch-all
description: anti-pattern cluster [catch-all]: 53 findings across code/packages/excalidraw/subset/woff2/woff2-bindings.ts(6) code/packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/useTTDChatStorage.ts(3) code/packages/common/src/utils.ts(3) — sample: bare catch-all with no rethrow/reject/error-return/logging in body

name: anti-pattern-console-only-error
description: anti-pattern cluster [console-only-error]: 87 findings across code/excalidraw-app/data/localStorage.ts(7) code/packages/excalidraw/data/restore.ts(6) code/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx(6) — sample: console.error with no UI feedback in surrounding context

name: anti-pattern-silent-catch
description: anti-pattern cluster [silent-catch]: 55 findings across code/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx(6) code/excalidraw-app/data/localStorage.ts(6) code/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts(3) — sample: catch body logs error but never surfaces to user

name: anti-pattern-todo-density
description: anti-pattern cluster [todo-density]: 9 findings across code/packages/math/src/point.ts(1) code/packages/excalidraw/types.ts(1) code/packages/excalidraw/tests/history.test.tsx(1) — sample: 4 debt markers in single file

name: anti-pattern-untested-churn
description: anti-pattern cluster [untested-churn]: 11 findings across code/setupTests.ts(1) code/scripts/woff2/woff2-vite-plugins.js(1) code/scripts/updateChangelog.js(1) — sample: 1 commits (6mo), no test file

name: anti-pattern-impact-scope
description: anti-pattern cluster [impact-scope]: 440 findings across code/scripts/updateChangelog.js(1) code/scripts/release.js(1) code/packages/utils/src/test-utils.ts(1) — sample: 158/247 importers of App have no tests (Tier 0: 247 direct callers, Tier 1: depth 2 transitive=905)

tsconfig.base.json ignoreDeprecations:"5.0" masked 5+ pre-existing TS errors — expected: suppresses only TS5.0-era deprecation warnings; got: when TS version bumps to 6.x, the string value "5.0" matches the new major version prefix and silently swallows unrelated errors across the workspace. Surface only emerges when the TS major version string matches the ignoreDeprecations value.
resolution: unresolved — candidates: remove ignoreDeprecations entirely (forces addressing all deprecated patterns), pin to a non-matching value, or migrate deprecated patterns; tracked in opus-audit-2026-05-04-post-wave4.md

description: Phase 1 typecheck baseline was never clean — atlas-app tsconfig paths:{} + tools rootDir isolation + vendored upstream errors combined to make full cross-workspace tsc appear green while individual package errors were masked. Expected: wave-gate typecheck exit 0 = monorepo-wide clean. Got: per-package tsc reported clean but cross-package references carried unresolved errors invisible to per-package runs.
resolution: identified in post-wave4 opus audit; requires: remove paths:{} override in atlas-app/tsconfig.json, fix rootDir in tools package, address vendored upstream errors or add project-reference exclusions

description: atlas-app/tsconfig.json paths:{} fully overrides inherited base paths — TS extends merges tsconfig keys shallowly: when a child declares paths:{}, the entire paths object from the base is discarded, not merged. Expected: child tsconfig extending base inherits base paths plus child paths. Got: child paths:{} wiped all base path aliases, causing resolution failures for any module the base had aliased.
resolution: remove or populate paths in atlas-app/tsconfig.json explicitly; do not use empty paths:{} as a no-op — it is a full override

content: anti-pattern detector must be scoped to atlasdraw-owned paths only — running detector across all of code/ sweeps vendored Excalidraw v0.18 (excalidraw-app, packages/{common,element,excalidraw,...}), generating 797 findings on code we don't own. Scope detector to: apps/atlas-app, packages/{tools,basemap,geo,atlasdraw-overlay}. Tracked under atlasdraw-d592.

description: husky postinstall expects code/.git that no longer exists — git was hoisted to repo root; husky 7.0.4 in code/package.json fails postinstall with exit-1. Non-blocking (deps install before postinstall hook runs) but causes yarn install to report failure even though node_modules land correctly.
resolution: verify artifacts directly (ls node_modules/@types/geojson) before assuming install failed. Long-term fix: update husky config or remove from code/package.json. Tracked under atlasdraw-0c97.

content: LSP diagnostic noise during background worker writes is ignorable: LSP shows phantom syntax errors at lines beyond actual EOF (e.g. useAtlasdrawTool.ts 312 lines, LSP claimed errors at 313-330). Source: stale buffer or progressive-write reads. Source of truth for verification: git status + actual test runs + build. NEVER LSP diagnostics.

description: yarn workspace add lockfile race — @turf/distance install pruned vite-plugin-checker hoisting; build broke with missing module. Expected: workspace add leaves hoisted packages intact. Got: pruned dep, build failure.
resolution: ran bare 'yarn install' to re-hoist; build restored. Future: run yarn install immediately after any workspace add, verify build before committing.

description: vitest globals:false defeats RTL automatic cleanup — expected: React Testing Library's afterEach(cleanup) fires automatically when globals:true; got: with globals:false (atlasdraw workspace vitest configs), globalThis.afterEach is undefined at RTL module evaluation, so automatic cleanup never registers. Surfaced in TWO workspaces this wave: T12 (LayerPanel tests in atlas-app) and T14 (MapEditor contextmenu tests). Pattern: any workspace where vitest.config has globals:false must add an explicit afterEach(cleanup) import in either the test file or a setupFiles entry.
resolution: Add explicit import { afterEach } from 'vitest'; import { cleanup } from '@testing-library/react'; afterEach(cleanup); to test setup. Applies to: code/apps/atlas-app and code/packages/tools — both have globals:false. Confirmed by T12 + T14 both hitting same failure class independently.

content: CLI tsconfig must include ../data/src/shpjs.d.ts to compile — when @atlasdraw/cli imports from @atlasdraw/data and the data barrel pulls in shapefile.ts (which references shpjs ambient types), the CLI's tsc resolution needs the same ambient declaration. T10 added '../data/src/shpjs.d.ts' to code/packages/cli/tsconfig.json include[]. Will reappear in any new package consuming @atlasdraw/data.

CLI base tsconfig needs "types":["node"] when using node globals — base tsconfig has no types field, so @types/node does not auto-load even when present in node_modules. Symptom: TS2591 'Cannot find name process'. Bench tsconfig already had types:["vitest/globals","node"]. Fix: add types:["node"] to CLI tsconfig compilerOptions. Applies to any new package that uses process, Buffer, __dirname, etc.

vitest 3.0.6 dropped --reporter=basic — use default reporter. The --reporter=basic flag emits startup error 'Failed to load custom Reporter from basic'. Companion to prior known issue with --testPathPattern (now --reporter dropped too). Safe invocation: vitest run <file> (no --reporter flag).
